warnings: [dmn_audit] The artifact contains 9 decision definitions and 1 router decision. The router uses variables (is_priority_exit, mod_*_done flags) that are not defined as inputData elements; they appear to be intended as inter-decision state variables but are not explicitly wired.
[dmn_audit] Multiple decisions reference the same inputData IDs (e.g., demo_age_months, q_blood_in_stool) but these are only defined once in the artifact, which is correct for shared inputs.
[dmn_audit] The decision table rules use complex boolean expressions with logical operators (&&, ||, ==, !=, <, >, >=, <=) which are valid DMN FEEL syntax.
[dmn_audit] No aggregate decision (KPI or scorecard) wiring detected; all decisions are independent or reference other decisions via informationRequirement.
[dmn_audit] ID uniqueness check: all element IDs appear unique within their scope (no duplicate ids detected).

[boundary_condition] Router rule_router_0 uses is_priority_exit flag but this flag is not consistently set across all modules. Some modules (e.g., mod_diarrhoea_treatment) do not have is_priority_exit output defined. Short-circuit may not trigger reliably.

[boundary_condition] Vaccine age boundaries use both demo_age_in_weeks and demo_age_in_months in same table (dt_mod_check_vaccines). Mixing units increases risk of unit conversion errors. Recommend standardizing to single unit.

[boundary_condition] MUAC color thresholds (red < 115, yellow 115-124, green >= 125) are hardcoded in multiple rules across multiple tables. No centralized definition. If threshold changes, multiple rules must be updated, increasing error risk.

[boundary_condition] Fever RDT result is used in dt_mod_fever_malaria_treatment but RDT is only performed if fever < 7 days in malaria area. If RDT not performed, lab_rdt_result may be null, causing rule matching to fail silently. No explicit null-handling rule.
[boundary_condition] Diarrhoea treatment rules do not explicitly handle case where q_diarrhoea_days is null/missing. Rules assume duration is always provided if q_has_diarrhoea == true.

[boundary_condition] Fast breathing respiratory rate thresholds (50 bpm for 2-12mo, 40 bpm for 12-60mo) are embedded in rule_2 of dt_mod_assess but not explicitly tested in dt_mod_fast_breathing_treatment. Inconsistency risk.
[boundary_condition] Home care advice module (dt_mod_home_care_advice) has 15 rules with complex nested conditions. No default catch-all rule; if no rule matches, output is undefined.

[boundary_condition] Router rule_router_0 uses is_priority_exit flag for short-circuit emergency exit. Verify that all clinical modules set is_priority_exit = true when danger signs are detected, to ensure emergency pathway is not bypassed.

[boundary_condition] Diarrhoea treatment rules (rule_5 and rule_6) depend on age thresholds (2-6 months vs 6 months to < 5 years). Boundary at 6 months: verify that a child at exactly 6 months matches rule_6 (>= 6) not rule_5 (< 6).

[boundary_condition] Fever/malaria treatment rules use age thresholds at 2-35 months vs 36-59 months. Boundary at 36 months: verify that a child at exactly 36 months matches rules for 36-59 months (>= 36) not 2-35 months (< 36).

[boundary_condition] Fast breathing treatment rules use age thresholds at 2-12 months vs 12 months to < 5 years. Boundary at 12 months: verify that a child at exactly 12 months matches rule_5 (>= 12) not rule_4 (< 12).

[boundary_condition] Malnutrition screening applies only to age 6 months to < 5 years. Children < 6 months or >= 60 months are excluded (rule_6). Verify this is intentional and that no malnutrition assessment is needed outside this range.

[boundary_condition] Home care advice module has 15 rules with complex condition combinations. Verify that all possible combinations of (danger_sign, referral, illness_treated, malnutrition) are covered and no gaps exist.

[boundary_condition] Router uses PRIORITY_EXIT as a short-circuit output. Verify that the calling system recognizes this output and immediately routes to emergency referral without executing remaining modules.

[boundary_condition] The dt_mod_check_vaccines table has UNIQUE hit policy but contains multiple 'due' rules for each age band without corresponding 'up-to-date' rules. This creates coverage gaps where a child with all vaccines received at a given age will not match any rule, leading to undefined output.

[boundary_condition] The dt_mod_assess table is very large (9 rules) with complex nested boolean expressions. Boundary testing of all combinations is difficult; recommend simplification or explicit default catch-all rule.

[boundary_condition] The dt_mod_danger_sign_check rule_4 uses null checks (q_diarrhoea_days == null OR q_diarrhoea_days < 14) which may not be portable across all DMN engines; consider explicit 'no diarrhoea' input instead.
[boundary_condition] The dt_mod_fever_malaria_treatment rules 2-5 use age thresholds at 36 months (2-35 vs 36-59) which is a boundary; verify that age exactly 36 months is correctly classified.

[boundary_condition] The dt_mod_malnutrition_screening rule_6 excludes age < 6 and >= 60 months but does not explicitly output a 'not assessed' flag; output p_malnutrition_not_assessed may be undefined for out-of-range ages.

[boundary_condition] Router rule_router_8 is a catch-all (true condition) that always matches. This is correct for a default fallback, but ensure no critical module is unreachable due to earlier rules consuming all inputs. Verify that mod_diarrhoea_treatment, mod_fast_breathing_treatment, and mod_fever_malaria_treatment are only routed when their respective conditions (q_has_diarrhoea, p_fast_breathing, q_has_fever) are true and their _done flags are false.

[boundary_condition] Vaccine module (dt_mod_check_vaccines) has 13 rules covering age ranges 0-6 weeks, 6-10 weeks, 10-14 weeks, 14 weeks to 9 months, 9-18 months, and 18 months to 5 years. Confirm that all age boundaries are mutually exclusive and exhaustive within the 0-60 month range. Gap detected: ages 0-2 months are not explicitly covered if demo_age_in_weeks is not always available; verify input data availability.

[boundary_condition] Diarrhoea treatment module (dt_mod_diarrhoea_treatment) rules 5 and 6 use age thresholds 2-6 months and 6-60 months. Confirm that a child at exactly 6 months is routed to rule_6 (>= 6 months), not rule_5 (< 6 months). The boundary at 6 months is correctly handled by rule_5 using < 6 and rule_6 using >= 6.

[boundary_condition] Fast breathing treatment module (dt_mod_fast_breathing_treatment) rules 4 and 5 use age thresholds 2-12 months and 12-60 months. Confirm that a child at exactly 12 months is routed to rule_5 (>= 12 months), not rule_4 (< 12 months). The boundary at 12 months is correctly handled.

[boundary_condition] Fever/malaria treatment module (dt_mod_fever_malaria_treatment) rules 2-7 use age thresholds 2-35 months and 36-59 months. Confirm that a child at exactly 36 months is routed to the 36-59 month rule, not the 2-35 month rule. The boundary at 36 months is correctly handled by rule_5 using < 36 and rule_6 using >= 36.

[boundary_condition] Malnutrition screening module (dt_mod_malnutrition_screening) applies only to ages 6-60 months. Children < 6 months or >= 60 months are not assessed for MUAC-based malnutrition. Confirm this is intentional and that alternative malnutrition assessment is available for infants < 6 months.
[boundary_condition] dt_mod_check_vaccines has 13 rules but no explicit catch-all or default row for children >= 60 months or < 2 weeks. Verify coverage is intentional.
[boundary_condition] dt_mod_assess has 9 rules with UNIQUE hit policy. Verify no overlapping conditions can fire simultaneously.
[boundary_condition] dt_mod_danger_sign_check has 4 rules with UNIQUE hit policy. Verify no overlapping conditions can fire simultaneously.
[boundary_condition] dt_mod_diarrhoea_treatment has 6 rules with UNIQUE hit policy. Verify no overlapping conditions can fire simultaneously.
[boundary_condition] dt_mod_fast_breathing_treatment has 5 rules with UNIQUE hit policy. Verify no overlapping conditions can fire simultaneously.
[boundary_condition] dt_mod_fever_malaria_treatment has 12 rules with UNIQUE hit policy. Verify no overlapping conditions can fire simultaneously.
[boundary_condition] dt_mod_malnutrition_screening has 6 rules with UNIQUE hit policy. Verify no overlapping conditions can fire simultaneously.
[boundary_condition] dt_router has 8 rules with PRIORITY hit policy. Rule rule_router_8 (true) is a catch-all that will always match; verify this is intentional as final fallback.

The artifact contains 9 decision definitions and 1 router decision. The router uses variables (is_priority_exit, mod_*_done flags) that are not defined as inputData elements; they appear to be intended as inter-decision state variables but are not explicitly wired.
Multiple decisions reference the same inputData IDs (e.g., demo_age_months, q_blood_in_stool) but these are only defined once in the artifact, which is correct for shared inputs.
The decision table rules use complex boolean expressions with logical operators (&&, ||, ==, !=, <, >, >=, <=) which are valid DMN FEEL syntax.
No aggregate decision (KPI or scorecard) wiring detected; all decisions are independent or reference other decisions via informationRequirement.
ID uniqueness check: all element IDs appear unique within their scope (no duplicate ids detected).

Router rule_router_0 uses is_priority_exit flag but this flag is not consistently set across all modules. Some modules (e.g., mod_diarrhoea_treatment) do not have is_priority_exit output defined. Short-circuit may not trigger reliably.

Vaccine age boundaries use both demo_age_in_weeks and demo_age_in_months in same table (dt_mod_check_vaccines). Mixing units increases risk of unit conversion errors. Recommend standardizing to single unit.

MUAC color thresholds (red < 115, yellow 115-124, green >= 125) are hardcoded in multiple rules across multiple tables. No centralized definition. If threshold changes, multiple rules must be updated, increasing error risk.

Fever RDT result is used in dt_mod_fever_malaria_treatment but RDT is only performed if fever < 7 days in malaria area. If RDT not performed, lab_rdt_result may be null, causing rule matching to fail silently. No explicit null-handling rule.

Fast breathing respiratory rate thresholds (50 bpm for 2-12mo, 40 bpm for 12-60mo) are embedded in rule_2 of dt_mod_assess but not explicitly tested in dt_mod_fast_breathing_treatment. Inconsistency risk.
